subject: UX & Research
title: Google's HEART Framework
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Google's HEART Framework.

hook.text: Google needed a UX metrics system that worked across Gmail, Maps, and Search. They created HEART.

buildup.text: HEART: Happiness, Engagement, Adoption, Retention, Task success. Five dimensions that cover the full UX picture.

discovery.text: For each dimension, define Goals, Signals, and Metrics. GSM turns vague wishes into trackable numbers.

twist.text: Teams don't need all five. Pick 2-3 dimensions that matter for your product stage. Focus beats coverage.

climax.text: HEART gives teams a shared language for UX quality. Everyone argues about the same metrics, not different ones.

punchline.text: Five dimensions, pick your focus. HEART gives a shared UX language.

quiz.question: What does HEART stand for in Google's UX framework?
options: Happiness, Engagement, Adoption, Retention, Task success
